project.name: safeskoolz
project.description: Multi-role safeguarding and incident reporting platform for schools, compliant with LOPIVI, Convivèxit 2024, and Machista Violence protocols. Powered by Cloudworkz.
project.tagline: A safe space to speak up and seek help.
legalFrameworks: LOPIVI (Ley Orgánica de Protección Integral a la Infancia y la Adolescencia)

files.src/app.ts: Express app setup, CORS, rate limiting, middleware registration
files.src/index.ts: Server bootstrap, cron scheduling for pattern detection
files.src/routes/index.ts: Route aggregator — mounts all route files under /api prefix
files.src/routes/auth.ts: Authentication routes (pupil PIN, staff email, parent email, demo login, profile)
files.src/routes/incidents.ts: Incident CRUD, consent workflow, assessment, status management
files.src/routes/protocols.ts: Protocol CRUD with escalation tier calculation and mandatory referral engine
files.src/routes/alerts.ts: Pattern alert listing and status management
files.src/routes/dashboard.ts: Coordinator, teacher, parent dashboards and analytics
files.src/routes/diagnostics.ts: Diagnostic survey CRUD, response submission, results, summary, actions, priorities
files.src/routes/diary.ts: Pupil diary CRUD with AI safeguarding scan
files.src/routes/messages.ts: Messaging system, conversations, safe contacts, child alerts
files.src/routes/behaviour.ts: Behaviour points CRUD, levels, pupil records, summary
files.src/routes/pta.ts: PTA portal — dashboard, messaging, policy, concerns, reports, codesign, mood trends, resources, parent-to-PTA
files.src/routes/caseTasks.ts: Case task management linked to protocols
files.src/routes/schools.ts: School listing, pupil listing, staff listing, pupil search, PIN reset, avatar management
files.src/routes/teacherPosts.ts: School updates (teacher posts) with audience filtering
files.src/routes/delegatedRoles.ts: Delegated safeguarding role assignment and revocation
files.src/routes/annexTemplates.ts: Annex template CRUD per compliance framework
files.src/lib/escalation.ts: Escalation tier determination and mandatory referral engine
files.src/lib/ptaPiiMiddleware.ts: PII stripping middleware for PTA role responses
files.src/lib/referenceNumber.ts: Unique reference number generator for incidents and protocols

files.src/App.tsx: Router configuration with ProtectedRoute wrapper and role guards
files.src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx: Sidebar navigation with role-specific menu items, dark mode toggle, notification badges, mobile bottom nav
files.src/components/demo/DemoWalkthrough.tsx: Interactive demo walkthrough overlay for new users
files.src/pages/login.tsx: Login page with school selector, pupil PIN pad, staff/parent email forms, demo login
files.src/pages/dashboard.tsx: Role router — dispatches to CoordinatorDashboard, TeacherDashboard, PupilDashboard, or ParentDashboard
files.src/pages/dashboard/CoordinatorDashboard: Full analytics dashboard with charts, PTA report management tab, delegated roles, recent incidents
files.src/pages/dashboard/TeacherDashboard.tsx: Teacher analytics scoped to class/year group
files.src/pages/dashboard/PupilDashboard.tsx: Pupil home with quick actions, behaviour overview, diary mood history
files.src/pages/dashboard/ParentDashboard.tsx: Parent dashboard with child incidents, emotional timeline, school overview, PTA contact card
files.src/pages/report-incident.tsx: Dual-mode incident form — child-friendly (pupils) vs professional (staff)
files.src/pages/incidents.tsx: Incident list with filtering, search, pagination; incident detail with consent workflow
files.src/pages/diagnostics.tsx: Diagnostic survey taking UI with role-adaptive question rendering
files.src/pages/diagnostics-results.tsx: Full results (coordinator) or published summary (others) with charts and priorities
files.src/pages/diary.tsx: Pupil feelings diary with physical diary aesthetic, mood emoji picker, leather-look cover
files.src/pages/behaviour.tsx: Behaviour tracker — staff can issue points, pupils see own record, parents see child record
files.src/pages/messages.tsx: Messaging with conversations, compose, safe contacts (pupils), urgent help
files.src/pages/pta.tsx: PTA portal with 6 tabs: Dashboard, Messages, Policy, Report, CoDesign, Resources
files.src/pages/caseload.tsx: SENCO caseload management with tracking forms and progress charts
files.src/pages/learnings.tsx: School updates feed with compose form (staff), category filters, Heads Up banner
files.src/pages/my-class.tsx: Class/year group pupil list with behaviour status and incident history links
files.src/pages/education.tsx: Rights education page — UNCRC, LOPIVI, Convivèxit, Machista Violence protocol accordions
files.src/pages/training.tsx: Role-specific training guides with step-by-step walkthroughs
files.src/pages/alerts.tsx: Pattern alerts list with review actions
files.src/pages/settings.tsx: User settings — avatar, profile, dark mode, language preferences
files.src/pages/newsletter.tsx: Public newsletter signup form for organisations

lib/db.description: Drizzle ORM schema definitions + PostgreSQL connection

structure.lib/api-client-react: Auto-generated React Query hooks via Orval from OpenAPI spec
structure.lib/api-zod: Auto-generated Zod validation schemas from OpenAPI spec
structure.scripts/src/seed.ts: Database seeding script for demo data

backend.ai: OpenAI SDK via Replit AI Integrations proxy

logic: Validates pupil exists in school with role='pupil' and active=true
Max 3 failed attempts → locked until 2099-12-31 (permanent lockout)
firstLogin = true if user.lastLogin was null before this request
Only allows roles: teacher, head_of_year, coordinator, head_teacher, senco, support_staff, pta
Fetches first active user matching requested role
Returns current user from JWT userId

jwtConfig.secret: JWT_SECRET env var (throws on startup if missing)

middleware.authMiddleware: Extracts Bearer token from Authorization header, verifies JWT, attaches decoded payload to (req as any).user
middleware.requireRole: Higher-order function — returns middleware that checks req.user.role against allowed roles array, returns 403 if no match

routes.access: coordinator, head_teacher, senco, head_of_year, teacher, parent

visibilityRules.parent: Only parentVisible=true incidents where their child is victim or perpetrator
visibilityRules.teacher: Only incidents involving pupils in their assigned class
visibilityRules.head_of_year: Only incidents involving pupils in their assigned year group

routes.enrichment: enrichIncidents maps user IDs to names and redacts sensitive fields based on viewer role

logic: Generates unique reference number via generateIncidentRef()
Calls determineEscalationTier() to calculate severity tier (1-3)
Calls isSafeguardingTrigger() to check for critical categories
If tier >= 3 or safeguarding trigger → immediately notifies all active coordinators/head teachers via in-app notification
Sends in-app confirmation notification to reporter (if not anonymous)

accessControl.pupil: Can only view reports they filed
accessControl.parent: Can view if they are reporter OR if parentVisible=true and involves their child
accessControl.teacher/head_of_year/support_staff: Limited to class/year group unless linked to accessible pattern alert

routes.consentMasking: For teacher, head_of_year, support_staff — description, victimIds, and sensitive fields masked unless teacherConsentStatus='approved'

logic: Audit log: incident_status_updated event with new status and note
Teachers can only assess incidents involving their own pupils
If parentVisible set to true with parentSummary → auto-sends notification to parents of all involved victims
Validates parent's child is actually involved (victim or perpetrator)
Once 'approved', teachers see full unredacted incident details

routes.response: Protocol[] enriched with victimName and openedByName

logic: Generates unique reference number via generateProtocolRef()
Calls determineEscalationTier() to calculate tier based on incident category
Calls checkMandatoryReferral() — returns required=true if: tier 3, category='sexual'|'coercive', or linked to red-level repeat alert
Auto-populates externalReferralBody suggestion if mandatory (Sexual → 'Child Protection Services / Policía Nacional', Coercive → 'Child Protection Services / Social Services')

routes.response: Enriched protocol + linkedIncidents[] + interviews[] (with names) + notifications[] log

logic: Prevents closing if mandatory external referral is pending (externalReferralRequired=true but no externalReferralAt)
Sets closedAt if status changes to 'closed'
Sets reviewedAt and reviewedBy on status change

routes.response: Anonymised school-wide statistics for parent transparency

questionBank.roleTargeting: Questions have role-specific display text (pupil version vs staff version vs parent version)

prioritySystem.perceptionGap: Difference between highest and lowest scoring role groups — flagged as alignmentNote when gap ≥ 1.5
prioritySystem.ranking: Sorted by urgency (critical first), then by lowest overallAvg

kpiMap.description: Each category maps to specific KPIs with baseline/target/timeframe and suggested actions

logic: Only accepts question keys valid for user's role

response.priorities: [{category, urgency, overallAvg, perceptionGap, kpis, suggestedActions}] ranked by urgency

logic: Non-leadership roles only see actions where publishedAt IS NOT NULL
Non-leadership roles only see published actions
Sets publishedAt on ALL actions for this survey, making them visible to non-leadership roles

routes.access: coordinator, head_teacher (dev only — gated to non-production)
logic: Generates synthetic responses for all users in school
Uses role-specific 'profiles' with means and spreads for realistic distribution
Strictly filtered to pupil's own entries only — no other role can access
Triggers async (non-blocking) AI safeguarding scan via scanDiaryEntry()
AI scan only runs if note exists and length ≥ 10 characters
Uses OpenAI gpt-5-nano model via Replit AI Integrations proxy
AI system prompt defines strict flagging criteria: ONLY flag self-harm, abuse, violence, exploitation, neglect, grooming
AI must NOT flag normal sadness, friend arguments, homework stress, feeling left out
AI responds with JSON: {flag: boolean, level: 'red'|'amber'|null, reason: string|null}
If flagged → creates pattern_alert with SAFE MESSAGE ('A pupil's diary entry has been flagged for welfare review') — NO diary text in alert
Red flags → notifies coordinator and head_teacher
Amber flags → notifies coordinator and SENCO
If AI unavailable or errors → silently fails (no user impact)
Verifies entry belongs to requesting pupil before deletion

aiSafeguardingConfig.provider: OpenAI SDK via Replit AI Integrations
aiSafeguardingConfig.noTemperature: temperature parameter not supported for this model
flagCriteria: Physical abuse or violence at home
Severe bullying with threats of harm
aiSafeguardingConfig.privacyGuarantee: Diary text is NEVER included in alerts, logs, or any external-facing data

routes.response: Aggregated totalPoints and current level for all pupils in school
Point entry with recalculated totalPoints and level

logic: Creates notification for recipient with auto-generated subject/body based on message type

routes.response: [urgent_help messages sent by parent's children, with childName, recipientName, recipientRole]
[staff members with isFormTutor boolean and child-friendly displayRole (e.g. 'School Counsellor')]

piiMiddleware.description: Intercepts all /pta/* responses for users with role='pta'
piiMiddleware.mechanism: Recursively walks JSON response body, removes any key matching PII_FIELDS Set

response.behaviourDistribution: [{level name, count}] — classifies students into behaviour levels by accumulated points

routes.note: All data anonymised via PII middleware — no student/staff names exposed

response.policy: hardcoded Safeguarding & Child Protection Policy metadata

logic: Records a 'flag' action_type — mandatory comment explaining concern
Updates accessedByPtaAt timestamp on first PTA view

routes.response: [all PTA annual reports for school, any status]

logic: Compiles academic year data: totalIncidents, incidentsByCategory, protocolsByStatus (open/resolved/closed counts), alertsSummary (total/amber/red/resolved counts)
Saves as 'draft' status in pta_annual_reports table
Once approved, visible to PTA members via /report/latest
Queries pupil_diary entries from last 12 weeks
Privacy guard: HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT pupil_id) >= 5 — only returns data for weeks with ≥5 distinct pupils
Prevents de-anonymisation of individual student moods

response.questions: [predefined co-design questions e.g. 'Preferred language for parent notifications']

routes.response: [static resource list including 'LOPIVI Plain English Guide', 'Subject Access Request templates', etc.]
[PTA members with names partially masked (first name + last initial only, e.g. 'John D.')]

logic: Stored in pta_concerns table with category='parent_outreach'

routes.response: [CaseloadEntry[] with pupil details and latestTracking record]

logic: Validates pupil exists and is not a duplicate on caseload

routes.response: [pupils in school NOT currently on requester's active caseload]

audienceFiltering.staff roles: See posts with audience = everyone | staff
audienceFiltering.pupil: See posts with audience = everyone | pupils | pupils_parents
audienceFiltering.parent: See posts with audience = everyone | parents | pupils_parents

routes.access: staff roles only (teacher, head_of_year, support_staff, senco, coordinator, head_teacher)
routes.specialBehaviour: 'heads_up' category auto-sets audience to 'staff' regardless of what's submitted

logic: Only author or leadership can delete

routes.response: [{id, name}] — minimal data for login school selector
[{id, firstName, lastName, yearGroup, className, avatarType, avatarValue}] — minimal privacy-preserving data for pupil PIN login selector
[pupils in teacher's class or year group]

logic: Resets all specified pupils to same PIN

routes.response: [Notification[] for current user, ordered by sent_at DESC]
Always returns 200 with success message (prevents email enumeration)

patternDetectionEngine.execution: Cron-scheduled every 60 minutes via setInterval in server startup + triggered on incident creation

rules.label: Escalating Pattern
rules.trigger: Same victim involved in multiple incidents of different categories

rules.label: Group Targeting
rules.trigger: Same victim targeted by multiple different perpetrators

rules.label: Repeat Victim
rules.trigger: Same victim involved in multiple incidents (fallback if not escalating/group targeting)

rules.label: Repeat Perpetrator
rules.trigger: Same perpetrator involved in multiple incidents

rules.label: Location Hotspot
rules.trigger: Multiple incidents at the same specific location

rules.label: Emotional Distress Pattern
rules.trigger: Victim reports 'scared', 'sad', or 'worried' emotional states across incidents

rules.label: Mood Decline Scan
rules.trigger: Scheduled scan of pupil_diary entries for sustained low wellbeing
rules.note: This rule scans diary data, not incidents — runs on cron only

deduplication.mechanism: Before creating alert, checks for existing 'open' alert with same ruleId + schoolId + victimId
deduplication.result: If match found → skips creation (returns false), preventing alert fatigue

triggers: Linked to a repeating red-level pattern alert
mandatoryReferral.enforcement: Protocol cannot be closed while mandatory external referral is pending (externalReferralRequired=true but no externalReferralAt)

authentication.secret: JWT_SECRET env var (mandatory — server crashes on startup if missing)

lockout.resetOnSuccess: failed_login_attempts set to 0 on successful login

authentication.pupilSessionTimeout: 90 seconds inactivity → auto-logout (frontend timer, pupil role only)
authentication.rateLimiting: express-rate-limit on auth and newsletter endpoints

dataProtection: Cross-tenant enforcement: ALL database queries filter by schoolId from JWT — no cross-school data leakage
IDOR prevention: All :id param endpoints verify resource belongs to requesting user's school
PTA PII middleware: Intercepts JSON responses for role='pta', recursively strips 40+ named PII fields
Immutable audit logs: PostgreSQL trigger prevents UPDATE and DELETE on audit_log table
Teacher consent: Incident description, victimIds, sensitive fields masked for teacher/head_of_year/support_staff until parent explicitly approves
Diary privacy: Pupil diary entries NEVER exposed in alerts, logs, API responses to other roles, or AI-generated alerts
AI safeguarding alerts use fixed safe message: 'A pupil's diary entry has been flagged for welfare review' — no diary text included
Mood trends privacy: PTA mood-trends endpoint requires ≥5 distinct pupils per week (HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT pupil_id) >= 5)
Privacy-conscious pupil search: Limited result fields returned
Newsletter enumeration prevention: POST /newsletter/subscribe always returns 200 regardless of duplicate
Sensitive fields never returned: passwordHash, pinHash stripped by formatUser() helper
PTA contacts: Parents see only first name + last initial of PTA members (e.g. 'John D.')
security.cors: Restricted origins configured in Express app

audit_log.immutability: PostgreSQL trigger prevents UPDATE and DELETE operations

publicRoutes.description: School selector → role-based login (PIN pad for pupils, email forms for staff/parent)

protectedRoutes.description: Role router → dispatches to CoordinatorDashboard, TeacherDashboard, PupilDashboard, or ParentDashboard
Incident list with filtering, search, pagination
Incident detail with consent workflow and assessment form
Protocol detail with interviews, notifications, linked incidents
Rights education — UNCRC, LOPIVI, Convivèxit, Machista Violence
Results (full or summary based on role)

seededDemoData.pupils: ~20 pupils across 4 year groups with animal emoji avatars

diagnosticSurveys.note: With seeded responses from all role groups
With seeded responses from all role groups

seededDemoData.incidents: 169 seeded incidents across all categories and escalation tiers
seededDemoData.patternAlerts: 40 seeded pattern alerts (mix of red and amber)

internalHelpers.formatUser: Strips passwordHash, pinHash, and sensitive fields from user object before API response
internalHelpers.enrichIncidents: Maps user IDs to names, redacts sensitive fields based on viewer role
internalHelpers.enrichProtocols: Adds victimName and openedByName to protocol objects
internalHelpers.generateIncidentRef: Generates unique incident reference number (e.g. INC-2025-001)
internalHelpers.writeAudit: Writes to immutable audit_log table. Signature: writeAudit({schoolId, eventType, actor, targetType, targetId, details, req})
internalHelpers.getRoleGroup: Collapses specific roles into 3 reporting groups: pupil, parent (includes pta), staff (all others)
internalHelpers.getQuestionsForRole: Filters QUESTION_BANK for role-applicable questions with correct displayText
internalHelpers.scanDiaryEntry: Async AI safeguarding scan of diary note text via gpt-5-nano
